terms.definition: A systematic predisposition that favors certain interpretations or outcomes over others, thereby departing from statistical neutrality
The irreducible first-person phenomenological field within which experience appears; treated here as ontologically fundamental rather than emergent
A normative methodological stance that requires the explicit declaration of one’s foundational axioms or lenses prior to analysis
The network of unelected agencies exercising regulatory power within a constitutional framework
The metacognitive error of detecting bias more readily in others than in oneself
The conviction that one’s own perception is a direct, unbiased apprehension of reality
The tendency to over-rely on introspection as evidence of one’s own objectivity
The statistical concentration of like-minded political attitudes within an institution
The assertion that scientific reliability arises from a diversity of biases among scientists rather than from individual impartiality
A forthcoming 188-axiom meta-theoretical corpus integrating physics, theology, and information theory
